titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Victoria Mary Tyrrell 9, October, 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to his sister.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
9 October 17 My dear Minnie I was delighted to get your long
letters last night. I am glad that
some of the photographs were good
anyway. I am not surprised to hear that
they had lost trace of Hunter otherwise
he would have heard before. I think you may send me out
another suit if heavy flannels
as the weather might get cool any
time now. Of course there is not
the least hurry. It rains quite a lot at night now
but is usually very fine during
the day. I am glad Daddy got a couple of
pheasants. Yesterday we sent our man who
buys stuff for us and told him
to get some fish sole is possible
and two dozen eggs - He came
back in a great way and said they
had not got 2 dozen sole but
he could only get six and would
that be any use. He evidently
thought we wanted 2 dozen sole
too. Not bad for 4 people ! He
evidently does not realise cubic
capacity. No news. Everything is very
very quiet. With very best love to all
from
Your most affec brother
Willie
